questions.question: A train travels a distance of $480$ km at a uniform speed. If the speed of the train had been $8$ km/h less, then it would have taken $3$ hours more to cover the same distance. Let the uniform speed of the train be $x$ km/h. Form a quadratic equation in $x$ and solve it to find the speed of the train.
questions.answer: The speed of the train is $40$ km/h.

steps: Let the uniform speed of the train be $x$ km/h. The time taken to cover $480$ km is $T_1 = \frac{480}{x}$ hours.
If the speed is reduced by $8$ km/h, the new speed is $(x - 8)$ km/h. The new time taken is $T_2 = \frac{480}{x - 8}$ hours.
According to the problem, the difference in time is $3$ hours, so $T_2 - T_1 = 3$.
Simplify the equation by dividing by $3$ and finding a common denominator to get $x^2 - 8x - 1280 = 0$.
Solve the quadratic equation using the quadratic formula or middle-term splitting: $x^2 - 40x + 32x - 1280 = 0$.
Factorise to get $(x - 40)(x + 32) = 0$. Since speed cannot be negative, $x = 40$ km/h.
solution.derivation: $\frac{480}{x-8} - \frac{480}{x} = 3 \Rightarrow 480 \left( \frac{x - (x - 8)}{x(x - 8)} \right) = 3 \Rightarrow 480 \times 8 = 3x(x - 8) \Rightarrow 160 \times 8 = x^2 - 8x \Rightarrow 1280 = x^2 - 8x \Rightarrow x^2 - 8x - 1280 = 0 \Rightarrow x^2 - 40x + 32x - 1280 = 0 \Rightarrow x(x - 40) + 32(x - 40) = 0 \Rightarrow (x - 40)(x + 32) = 0 \Rightarrow x = 40 \text{ or } x = -32$

questions.subtopic: Word problems based on speed, distance, and time
